title: Acquisition rumor followed by listed-company denial
claim_to_evaluate: A deal blog said a listed industrial company had agreed to sell itself, but the company later denied it. How credible is the original takeover claim now?
baseline_goal: Train denial handling so the workflow gives proper weight to formal company responses without pretending denials always close the case.

sources.name: Deal Blog
sources.support: The outlet said a private-equity buyer had already reached board-level agreement.

sources.name: Exchange Filing
sources.support: The company said it had no disclosable signed agreement and warned investors to be careful.

sources.name: Second Press Report
sources.support: Another report said exploratory talks may have happened but were not finalized.

key_claims.claim: A takeover rumor was published and moved the stock.
A signed sale agreement had been confirmed by the company.
The right framing is that a signed deal was not confirmed, even if informal discussions may have existed.

credibility_reference.expected_judgment: Low confidence in a completed deal. Formal denial outweighs rumor until harder evidence appears.

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback if the output treats the initial rumor as stronger than the formal denial without new hard evidence.

notes.winner_pattern_hint: Give exchange-filed denial more weight than unsourced rumor while still noting talks may have existed.
notes.loser_pattern_hint: Writing as if rumor plus price action equals confirmed acquisition.
